            np.mean(experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["sgg"]["lam_0.3"][-5:])
        )
        sgg_improvement_matrix[si, di] = sgg_base - sgg_sem
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
im = ax.imshow(
    sgg_improvement_matrix, cmap="RdYlGn", aspect="auto", vmin=-0.2, vmax=0.2
)
ax.set_xticks(range(n_ds))
ax.set_xticklabels(ds_list, fontsize=9)
ax.set_yticks(range(n_strategies))
ax.set_yticklabels(COVERAGE_STRATEGIES)
for i in range(n_strategies):
    for j in range(n_ds):
        ax.text(
            j,
            i,
            f"{sgg_improvement_matrix[i, j]:.3f}",
            ha="center",
            va="center",
            fontsize=10,
            color="black",
        )
plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label="SGG Improvement (positive=semantic reward helped)")
ax.set_title(
    "SGG Improvement from Semantic Reward\nby Test Coverage Density Strategy × Dataset"
)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, "sgg_improvement_heatmap.png"), dpi=100)
plt.close()
print("Saved: sgg_improvement_heatmap.png")

# Plot 6: Held-out pass rate heatmap (lam=0.3)
held_matrix = np.zeros((n_strategies, n_ds))
for si, strategy in enumerate(COVERAGE_STRATEGIES):
    for di, ds_name in enumerate(ds_list):
        held_matrix[si, di] = float(
            np.mean(experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["held"]["lam_0.3"][-5:])
        )
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
im = ax.imshow(held_matrix, cmap="RdYlGn", aspect="auto", vmin=0, vmax=1)
ax.set_xticks(range(n_ds))
ax.set_xticklabels(ds_list, fontsize=9)
ax.set_yticks(range(n_strategies))
ax.set_yticklabels(COVERAGE_STRATEGIES)
for i in range(n_strategies):
    for j in range(n_ds):
        ax.text(
            j,
            i,
            f"{held_matrix[i, j]:.3f}",
            ha="center",
            va="center",
            fontsize=10,
            color="black",
        )
plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label="Held-out Pass Rate (higher=better)")
ax.set_title("Held-out Pass Rate (λ=0.3)\nby Test Coverage Density Strategy × Dataset")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, "held_out_heatmap_coverage.png"), dpi=100)
plt.close()
print("Saved: held_out_heatmap_coverage.png")

# Plot 7: Number of given tests per strategy per dataset
n_given_matrix = np.zeros((n_strategies, n_ds))
for si, strategy in enumerate(COVERAGE_STRATEGIES):
    for di, ds_name in enumerate(ds_list):
        ds_raw = DATASET_CONFIGS[ds_name]
        counts = []
        for p in ds_raw:
            np_ = apply_coverage_strategy(p, strategy)
            if np_ is not None and np_.get("given_tests"):
                counts.append(len(np_["given_tests"]))
        n_given_matrix[si, di] = np.mean(counts) if counts else 0
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
im = ax.imshow(n_given_matrix, cmap="Blues", aspect="auto")
ax.set_xticks(range(n_ds))
ax.set_xticklabels(ds_list, fontsize=9)
ax.set_yticks(range(n_strategies))
ax.set_yticklabels(COVERAGE_STRATEGIES)
for i in range(n_strategies):
    for j in range(n_ds):
        ax.text(
            j,
            i,
            f"{n_given_matrix[i, j]:.1f}",
            ha="center",
            va="center",
            fontsize=10,
            color="black",
        )
plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label="Avg # Given Tests")
ax.set_title("Average Number of Given Tests\nby Coverage Strategy × Dataset")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, "n_given_tests_heatmap.png"), dpi=100)
plt.close()
print("Saved: n_given_tests_heatmap.png")

# Summary
print("\n=== Final Results Summary: Test Coverage Density Ablation ===")
print(
    f"{'Dataset':<18} {'Strategy':<12} {'λ':<8} {'Final SGG':<12} {'Held-out PR':<14} {'SGG Improv':<12}"
)
print("-" * 80)
for ds_name in ds_list:
    for strategy in COVERAGE_STRATEGIES:
        sgg_b = float(
            np.mean(experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["sgg"]["lam_0.0"][-5:])
        )
        held_b = float(
            np.mean(experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["held"]["lam_0.0"][-5:])
        )
        sgg_s = float(
            np.mean(experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["sgg"]["lam_0.3"][-5:])
        )
        held_s = float(
            np.mean(experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["held"]["lam_0.3"][-5:])
        )
        improvement = sgg_b - sgg_s
        print(
            f"{ds_name:<18} {strategy:<12} {'0.0':<8} {sgg_b:<12.4f} {held_b:<14.4f} {'':<12}"
        )
        print(
            f"{ds_name:<18} {strategy:<12} {'0.3':<8} {sgg_s:<12.4f} {held_s:<14.4f} {improvement:<12.4f}"
        )
    print()

print(
    f"\nDiscriminator: val_acc={disc_val_acc[-1]:.4f}, val_loss={disc_val_loss[-1]:.4f}"
)
print("\n=== Experiment Complete ===")
plot_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")

COVERAGE_STRATEGIES = experiment_data.get(
    "coverage_strategies", ["sparse", "medium", "dense", "adaptive"]
)
LAM_VALUES = experiment_data.get("lambda_values", [0.0, 0.3])
ds_list = ["hf_humaneval", "hf_mbpp", "hf_csn", "synthetic"]
strategy_colors = {
    "sparse": "red",
    "medium": "blue",
    "dense": "green",
    "adaptive": "purple",
}

# Plot 1: Discriminator training/validation curves
try:
    disc = experiment_data["discriminator"]
    train_loss = disc["train_loss"]
    val_loss = disc["val_loss"]
    val_acc = disc["val_acc"]
    epochs_disc = np.arange(1, len(train_loss) + 1)
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 5))
    axes[0].plot(epochs_disc, train_loss, label="Train Loss", color="blue")
    axes[0].plot(epochs_disc, val_loss, label="Val Loss", color="orange")
    axes[0].set_xlabel("Epoch")
    axes[0].set_ylabel("BCE Loss")
    axes[0].set_title("Discriminator: Train vs Val Loss")
    axes[0].legend()
    axes[0].grid(True)
    axes[1].plot(epochs_disc, val_acc, label="Val Accuracy", color="green")
    axes[1].set_xlabel("Epoch")
    axes[1].set_ylabel("Accuracy")
    axes[1].set_title("Discriminator: Validation Accuracy")
    axes[1].legend()
    axes[1].grid(True)
    plt.suptitle(
        "Semantic Discriminator Training Curves (All Datasets Combined)", fontsize=12
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, "discriminator_training_curves.png"), dpi=100)
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: discriminator_training_curves.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot1: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 2: SGG curves over RL epochs per dataset (lam=0.0 vs lam=0.3), all strategies
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(16, 12))
    axes = axes.flatten()
    for ax_i, ds_name in enumerate(ds_list):
        ax = axes[ax_i]
        for strategy in COVERAGE_STRATEGIES:
            sgg_03 = experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["sgg"]["lam_0.3"]
            sgg_00 = experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["sgg"]["lam_0.0"]
            epochs_rl = np.arange(1, len(sgg_03) + 1)
            ax.plot(
                epochs_rl,
                sgg_03,
                label=f"{strategy} λ=0.3",
                color=strategy_colors[strategy],
                linewidth=2,
            )
            ax.plot(
                epochs_rl,
                sgg_00,
                label=f"{strategy} λ=0.0",
                color=strategy_colors[strategy],
                linewidth=1,
                linestyle="--",
                alpha=0.5,
            )
        ax.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle=":", alpha=0.5)
        ax.set_xlabel("RL Epoch")
        ax.set_ylabel("SGG (Given - Held)")
        ax.set_title(f"SGG Over RL Epochs\nDataset: {ds_name}")
        ax.legend(fontsize=6, ncol=2)
        ax.grid(True)
    plt.suptitle(
        "SGG Curves by Coverage Strategy & Lambda (Lower SGG = Better Generalization)",
        fontsize=11,
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "sgg_curves_all_datasets_coverage.png"), dpi=100
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: sgg_curves_all_datasets_coverage.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot2: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 3: Held-out pass rate over RL epochs per dataset
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(16, 12))
    axes = axes.flatten()
    for ax_i, ds_name in enumerate(ds_list):
        ax = axes[ax_i]
        for strategy in COVERAGE_STRATEGIES:
            h_03 = experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["held"]["lam_0.3"]
            h_00 = experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["held"]["lam_0.0"]
            epochs_rl = np.arange(1, len(h_03) + 1)
            ax.plot(
                epochs_rl,
                h_03,
                label=f"{strategy} λ=0.3",
                color=strategy_colors[strategy],
                linewidth=2,
            )
            ax.plot(
                epochs_rl,
                h_00,
                label=f"{strategy} λ=0.0",
                color=strategy_colors[strategy],
                linewidth=1,
                linestyle="--",
                alpha=0.5,
            )
        ax.set_xlabel("RL Epoch")
        ax.set_ylabel("Held-out Pass Rate")
        ax.set_title(f"Held-out Pass Rate Over RL Epochs\nDataset: {ds_name}")
        ax.legend(fontsize=6, ncol=2)
        ax.grid(True)
        ax.set_ylim(-0.05, 1.1)
    plt.suptitle(
        "Held-out Pass Rate by Coverage Strategy & Lambda (Higher = Better)",
        fontsize=11,
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "held_out_pass_rate_all_datasets_coverage.png"),
        dpi=100,
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: held_out_pass_rate_all_datasets_coverage.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot3: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 4: Final SGG bar chart (last 5 epochs avg) comparing lambda=0.0 vs 0.3 per strategy per dataset
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(18, 7))
    n_ds = len(ds_list)
    n_strat = len(COVERAGE_STRATEGIES)
    width = 0.18
    x = np.arange(n_ds)
    for ax_idx, lam_key in enumerate(["lam_0.0", "lam_0.3"]):
        ax = axes[ax_idx]
        for si, strategy in enumerate(COVERAGE_STRATEGIES):
            vals = [
                float(np.mean(experiment_data[ds][strategy]["sgg"][lam_key][-5:]))
                for ds in ds_list
            ]
            offset = (si - n_strat / 2 + 0.5) * width
            bars = ax.bar(
                x + offset,
                vals,
                width,
                label=strategy,
                color=strategy_colors[strategy],
                alpha=0.8,
            )
            for bar in bars:
                h_val = bar.get_height()
                ax.text(
                    bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
                    h_val + 0.005,
                    f"{h_val:.3f}",
                    ha="center",
                    va="bottom",
                    fontsize=6,
                )
        ax.set_xticks(x)
        ax.set_xticklabels([d.replace("_", "\n") for d in ds_list], fontsize=9)
        ax.set_ylabel("Final SGG (last 5 epoch avg)")
        lam_label = (
            "λ=0.0 (Baseline, Execution Only)"
            if lam_key == "lam_0.0"
            else "λ=0.3 (Semantic Reward)"
        )
        ax.set_title(
            f"Final SGG by Coverage Strategy\n{lam_label} — Lower SGG = Better Generalization"
        )
        ax.legend(fontsize=9)
        ax.grid(True, axis="y")
        ax.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)
    plt.suptitle(
        "Final SGG Comparison: Baseline vs Semantic Reward Across Datasets & Coverage Strategies",
        fontsize=11,
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "final_sgg_bar_coverage_all_datasets.png"), dpi=100
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: final_sgg_bar_coverage_all_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot4: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 5: SGG Improvement heatmap (baseline SGG - semantic SGG; positive = semantic helped)
try:
    n_ds = len(ds_list)
    n_strat = len(COVERAGE_STRATEGIES)
    sgg_imp = np.zeros((n_strat, n_ds))
    for si, strategy in enumerate(COVERAGE_STRATEGIES):
        for di, ds_name in enumerate(ds_list):
            sgg_b = float(
                np.mean(experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["sgg"]["lam_0.0"][-5:])
            )
            sgg_s = float(
                np.mean(experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["sgg"]["lam_0.3"][-5:])
            )
            sgg_imp[si, di] = sgg_b - sgg_s
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
    im = ax.imshow(sgg_imp, cmap="RdYlGn", aspect="auto", vmin=-0.2, vmax=0.2)
    ax.set_xticks(range(n_ds))
    ax.set_xticklabels(ds_list, fontsize=9)
    ax.set_yticks(range(n_strat))
    ax.set_yticklabels(COVERAGE_STRATEGIES)
    for i in range(n_strat):
        for j in range(n_ds):
            ax.text(
                j,
                i,
                f"{sgg_imp[i, j]:.3f}",
                ha="center",
                va="center",
                fontsize=11,
                color="black",
            )
    plt.colorbar(
        im,
        ax=ax,
        label="SGG Improvement (positive = semantic reward reduced overfitting)",
    )
    ax.set_title(
        "SGG Improvement from Semantic Reward (λ=0.0 → λ=0.3)\nCoverage Strategy × Dataset — Positive Values = Semantic Reward Helped"
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "sgg_improvement_heatmap_coverage_datasets.png"),
        dpi=100,
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: sgg_improvement_heatmap_coverage_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot5: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 6: Held-out pass rate heatmap (lam=0.3)
try:
    n_ds = len(ds_list)
    n_strat = len(COVERAGE_STRATEGIES)
    held_mat = np.zeros((n_strat, n_ds))
    for si, strategy in enumerate(COVERAGE_STRATEGIES):
        for di, ds_name in enumerate(ds_list):
            held_mat[si, di] = float(
                np.mean(experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["held"]["lam_0.3"][-5:])
            )
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
    im = ax.imshow(held_mat, cmap="RdYlGn", aspect="auto", vmin=0, vmax=1)
    ax.set_xticks(range(n_ds))
    ax.set_xticklabels(ds_list, fontsize=9)
    ax.set_yticks(range(n_strat))
    ax.set_yticklabels(COVERAGE_STRATEGIES)
    for i in range(n_strat):
        for j in range(n_ds):
            ax.text(
                j,
                i,
                f"{held_mat[i, j]:.3f}",
                ha="center",
                va="center",
                fontsize=11,
                color="black",
            )
    plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label="Held-out Pass Rate (higher = better)")
    ax.set_title(
        "Held-out Pass Rate (λ=0.3, Semantic Reward)\nCoverage Strategy × Dataset"
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "held_out_heatmap_lam03_coverage_datasets.png"),
        dpi=100,
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: held_out_heatmap_lam03_coverage_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot6: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 7: Given vs Held-out pass rate comparison per dataset (final values, adaptive strategy)
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 6))
    for ax_idx, lam_key in enumerate(["lam_0.0", "lam_0.3"]):
        ax = axes[ax_idx]
        x = np.arange(len(ds_list))
        width = 0.35
        given_vals = [
            float(np.mean(experiment_data[ds]["adaptive"]["given"][lam_key][-5:]))
            for ds in ds_list
        ]
        held_vals = [
            float(np.mean(experiment_data[ds]["adaptive"]["held"][lam_key][-5:]))
            for ds in ds_list
        ]
        bars1 = ax.bar(
            x - width / 2,
            given_vals,
            width,
            label="Given (train) Pass Rate",
            color="steelblue",
            alpha=0.85,
        )
        bars2 = ax.bar(
            x + width / 2,
            held_vals,
            width,
            label="Held-out Pass Rate",
            color="tomato",
            alpha=0.85,
        )
        for bar in list(bars1) + list(bars2):
            ax.text(
                bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
                bar.get_height() + 0.01,
                f"{bar.get_height():.3f}",
                ha="center",
                va="bottom",
                fontsize=8,
            )
        ax.set_xticks(x)
        ax.set_xticklabels(ds_list, fontsize=9)
        ax.set_ylabel("Pass Rate")
        ax.set_ylim(0, 1.15)
        lam_label = (
            "λ=0.0 (Baseline)" if lam_key == "lam_0.0" else "λ=0.3 (Semantic Reward)"
        )
        ax.set_title(f"Adaptive Strategy: Given vs Held-out Pass Rate\n{lam_label}")
        ax.legend()
        ax.grid(True, axis="y")
    plt.suptitle(
        "Adaptive Coverage Strategy: Given vs Held-out Pass Rate Across Datasets",
        fontsize=11,
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "given_vs_held_adaptive_strategy_all_datasets.png"),
        dpi=100,
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: given_vs_held_adaptive_strategy_all_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot7: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Print summary metrics
try:
    print("\n=== Summary Metrics ===")
    disc = experiment_data["discriminator"]
    print(
        f"Discriminator final val_acc: {disc['val_acc'][-1]:.4f}, final val_loss: {disc['val_loss'][-1]:.4f}"
    )
    print(
        f"\n{'Dataset':<18} {'Strategy':<12} {'λ':<6} {'Final SGG':<12} {'Held-out PR':<14}"
    )
    print("-" * 65)
    for ds_name in ds_list:
        for strategy in COVERAGE_STRATEGIES:
            for lam_key in ["lam_0.0", "lam_0.3"]:
                sgg_val = float(
                    np.mean(experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["sgg"][lam_key][-5:])
                )
                held_val = float(
                    np.mean(experiment_data[ds_name][strategy]["held"][lam_key][-5:])
                )
                lam_str = lam_key.replace("lam_", "")
                print(
                    f"{ds_name:<18} {strategy:<12} {lam_str:<6} {sgg_val:<12.4f} {held_val:<14.4f}"
                )
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error summary: {e}")
plot_plan: The experiment trains a semantic discriminator to distinguish correct vs. adversarial code, then runs RL experiments across 4 datasets (hf_humaneval, hf_mbpp, hf_csn, synthetic) with 4 coverage strategies (sparse, medium, dense, adaptive) and 2 lambda values (0.0 baseline, 0.3 semantic reward). Key metrics are: discriminator train/val loss and accuracy, given-test pass rate, held-out pass rate, and SGG (given minus held, lower=better generalization). I'll plot the discriminator training curves, SGG curves over RL epochs per dataset, held-out pass rate curves, final SGG bar charts, SGG improvement heatmap, held-out pass rate heatmap, and a summary comparison. Each plot will be in its own try-except block with descriptive filenames.

plot_analyses.analysis:
Discriminator training curves (all datasets combined):
- Train/val BCE loss both drop rapidly from ~0.68/0.61 at epoch 1 to <0.05 by ~epoch 9–10, then asymptote near ~0.00 by ~epoch 20–30.
- Validation accuracy rises from ~0.945 to ~0.982 by ~epoch 5, reaches 1.00 around epoch ~12, and stays saturated thereafter.
Implications:
- The discriminator is extremely strong (near-perfect separability on its validation set). This can be good for providing a clean semantic signal, but it also raises red flags:
  (i) potential data leakage / overly-easy negatives / distribution mismatch between discriminator validation and RL-generated “gaming” behaviors;
  (ii) reward saturation: once the discriminator outputs near-binary scores, gradients/reward shaping may become brittle (policy learns to satisfy discriminator heuristics rather than semantics).
- Because both train and val losses go to ~0, there’s no visible overfitting in these curves, but the task may be too easy; you may want harder validation (fresh adversarial variants from current policy, paraphrase-held-out, or cross-dataset validation) and calibration plots (ECE, score histograms).

SGG curves by coverage strategy & λ (lower is better) across datasets:
- HumanEval: λ=0.0 (dashed) shows persistently high SGG, especially sparse (~0.25–0.45 range with spikes), indicating strong test-overfitting. With λ=0.3 (solid), SGG collapses toward ~0 by mid-to-late training for medium/dense/adaptive, and sparse also trends down to ~0 by ~epoch 19–21. This suggests semantic reward strongly reduces the given-vs-held-out gap.
- MBPP: Similar pattern. λ=0.0 dashed curves remain high (sparse often ~0.3–0.5). With λ=0.3, all strategies trend to ~0 SGG by ~epoch 19–21, with sparse taking longer and showing more volatility early.
- CSN: SGG is identically 0 for all strategies and both λ values across all epochs. This makes the metric non-informative here and is consistent with a pipeline issue (e.g., held-out == given, no held-out tests, or both pass rates are always 0).
- Synthetic: λ=0.0 dashed curves are high and erratic (often ~0.35–0.7), while λ=0.3 solid curves drop to ~0 by ~epoch 15–16 for all strategies.
Implications:
- The semantic reward (λ=0.3) appears to eliminate SGG across HumanEval/MBPP/synthetic, which is a very strong effect. Verify it’s not an artifact of how SGG is computed (e.g., clipping, averaging, or a bug where held-out is overwritten by given for λ>0).

Final SGG bar comparisons (λ=0.0 vs λ=0.3) across datasets & strategies:
- λ=0.0: HumanEval final SGG ~0.307 (sparse) vs ~0.172–0.181 (medium/dense) and ~0.174 (adaptive). MBPP final SGG ~0.447 (sparse) vs ~0.277–0.287 (others). Synthetic final SGG ~0.450 (sparse/adaptive) vs ~0.275 (medium/dense). CSN is 0 across the board.
- λ=0.3: HumanEval final SGG ~0.006 (sparse), ~0.000 (medium), ~0.013 (dense), ~0.013 (adaptive). MBPP final SGG ~0.007 for all strategies. Synthetic and CSN show 0.000.
Implications:
- Semantic reward reduces SGG by ~0.16–0.45 absolute depending on dataset/strategy, essentially driving it to ~0. This meets the stated success criterion (SGG < 0.05) easily.
- However, the near-identical MBPP SGG (~0.007) across strategies at λ=0.3 suggests the semantic reward dominates the effect of test coverage strategy; coverage becomes secondary once semantic reward is on. That’s an important ablation conclusion: with strong semantic shaping, you may not need dense/adaptive test coverage.
- The fact synthetic SGG becomes exactly 0.000 for all strategies at λ=0.3 is unusually clean; worth double-checking rounding/precision and whether last-5-epoch averaging includes many exact zeros due to held-out=given or both at ceiling.

Held-out pass rate over RL epochs (higher is better):
- HumanEval: λ=0.3 (solid) generally tracks high and improves over time, reaching ~0.95–0.98 by late epochs across strategies. λ=0.0 (dashed) lags lower (~0.6–0.85) with more variability.
- MBPP: λ=0.3 reaches ~0.99–1.00 by late epochs for all strategies. λ=0.0 remains around ~0.5–0.85 depending on strategy, with sparse baseline particularly low.
- CSN: held-out pass rate is 0.0 for all strategies and both λ values across all epochs.
- Synthetic: λ=0.3 reaches 1.0 by ~epoch 15 and stays there; λ=0.0 is substantially lower and unstable.
Implications:
- The semantic reward improves held-out pass rate dramatically, not just SGG. Combined with near-zero SGG, this indicates genuine generalization gains (assuming metrics are computed correctly).
- Ceiling effects: MBPP and synthetic saturate at ~1.0 held-out pass rate, which can mask differences between strategies/λ values and makes λ-sweeps harder to interpret. Consider harder held-out sets or more adversarial hidden tests to avoid saturation.
- CSN again indicates a systemic issue: either tasks are never solved (given pass also 0), or evaluation is broken.

SGG improvement heatmap (λ=0.0 → 0.3):
- HumanEval improvements: sparse +0.300, medium +0.172, dense +0.168, adaptive +0.161.
- MBPP improvements: sparse +0.440, medium +0.280, dense +0.280, adaptive +0.270.
- Synthetic improvements: sparse +0.450, medium +0.275, dense +0.275, adaptive +0.450.
- CSN: 0.000 everywhere.
Implications:
- Largest gains occur where baseline overfitting is worst (sparse coverage), consistent with the hypothesis that semantic reward counters reward hacking when tests are incomplete.
- Synthetic shows a striking pattern: sparse and adaptive gain more than medium/dense. Given adaptive has only ~1 test on synthetic (see test-count heatmap), it behaves like sparse there—consistent with the mechanism.
- CSN provides no signal; cannot be used to validate the hypothesis until fixed.

Held-out pass rate heatmap (λ=0.3):
- HumanEval: ~0.955–0.968 across strategies (small spread).
- MBPP: ~0.993 across all strategies (essentially identical).
- Synthetic: 1.000 across all strategies.
- CSN: 0.000 across all strategies.
Implications:
- Once λ=0.3 is enabled, coverage strategy differences largely vanish on MBPP/synthetic and are minor on HumanEval. This suggests (a) semantic reward is the dominant factor, and (b) you may be able to simplify the training recipe by using sparse tests + semantic reward.
- Again, saturation at 0.993–1.000 indicates the held-out suite may be too easy or too correlated with training tests; for a top-conference claim about robustness, you’ll want a harder “semantic generalization” split where held-out tests are materially different.

Average number of given tests heatmap:
- Sparse: 1.0 test everywhere.
- Medium: ~1.8 (HumanEval), 2.0 (MBPP), 3.0 (CSN), 2.9 (synthetic).
- Dense: ~2.8 (HumanEval), 2.0 (MBPP), 6.0 (CSN), 4.9 (synthetic).
- Adaptive: ~2.7 (HumanEval), 1.8 (MBPP), 4.8 (CSN), 1.0 (synthetic).
Implications:
- MBPP dense does not increase tests vs medium (both 2.0), so “dense” isn’t actually denser there; this can explain why strategy differences are small on MBPP even at λ=0.0.
- Adaptive on synthetic collapses to 1.0 test (same as sparse), explaining similar baseline SGG (~0.45) and similar improvement magnitude.
- CSN has the most tests under dense/adaptive, yet performance is 0; reinforces that CSN failure is not due to insufficient test coverage but likely format/eval/reward issues.

Adaptive strategy given vs held-out pass rate bars (λ=0.0 vs λ=0.3):
- λ=0.0: Given pass is very high (HumanEval ~0.968, MBPP 1.000, synthetic 1.000) but held-out is much lower (HumanEval ~0.794, MBPP ~0.723, synthetic ~0.550). Classic test-overfitting signature.
- λ=0.3: Given remains high (HumanEval ~0.968, MBPP 1.000, synthetic 1.000) and held-out jumps to near-given (HumanEval ~0.955, MBPP ~0.993, synthetic 1.000). CSN stays 0.
Implications:
- This is a clean demonstration of the core hypothesis: execution-only RL can drive given-test pass to ceiling while harming generalization; adding semantic reward closes the generalization gap without sacrificing given-test performance.
- For paper-quality evidence, replicate this plot for all strategies (not just adaptive) and include confidence intervals across seeds/tasks.

SGG by coverage strategy at λ=0.3 with baseline overlay:
- HumanEval/MBPP/synthetic: λ=0.3 curves sit far below the baseline (gray dashed medium, λ=0) across most epochs and approach 0 late in training.
- CSN: all curves flat at 0.
Implications:
- Confirms the time-course: semantic reward reduces overfitting progressively during RL, not just at the end.
- The baseline overlay is only medium strategy; adding overlays for sparse/dense/adaptive baselines would make the contrast clearer and avoid cherry-picking concerns.
- CSN remains the major blocker for “all datasets” claims; it should be excluded or fixed before aggregating results.

vlm_feedback_summary: Plots show (1) a discriminator that reaches near-zero BCE loss and ~100% validation accuracy by ~epoch 12, suggesting an extremely strong (possibly too-easy) semantic classifier; (2) adding semantic reward at λ=0.3 collapses SGG (given–held-out gap) to ~0 on HumanEval/MBPP/synthetic across coverage strategies while simultaneously boosting held-out pass rates to ~0.95–1.00, indicating a large reduction in test-overfitting; (3) coverage strategy effects largely vanish once semantic reward is enabled, and (4) CSN is pathological with SGG=0 and held-out pass=0 everywhere, indicating an evaluation/reward/data-format failure that must be diagnosed.
ablation_name: Test Coverage Density - Sparse vs Medium vs Dense vs Adaptive Test Sets

Ablation name: Reward Temporal Discounting - Immediate vs Delayed vs Exponential Decay Reward Signals.
The ablation study implements four temporal reward discounting schedules (flat, delayed, decay, growth) for the RL simulation, where the lambda weighting between execution and semantic rewards changes over training epochs. I'll adapt the baseline by adding `get_lambda_for_epoch()` and `combined_reward_dynamic()` functions, then run experiments across all three HuggingFace datasets for each schedule type. The results are saved as numpy arrays and visualized through multiple plots showing SGG curves, held-out pass rates, lambda schedules, bar charts, and heatmaps.

import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, Dataset
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import random
import re
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Using device: {device}")

random.seed(42)
np.random.seed(42)
torch.manual_seed(42)

from datasets import load_dataset

print("Loading HuggingFace datasets...")

try:
    humaneval_ds = load_dataset("openai_humaneval", split="test")
    humaneval_problems_raw = list(humaneval_ds)[:40]
    print(f"HumanEval loaded: {len(humaneval_problems_raw)} problems")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"HumanEval load error: {e}")
    humaneval_problems_raw = []

try:
    mbpp_ds = load_dataset("mbpp", split="test")
    mbpp_problems_raw = list(mbpp_ds)[:40]
    print(f"MBPP loaded: {len(mbpp_problems_raw)} problems")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"MBPP load error: {e}")
    mbpp_problems_raw = []

try:
    csn_ds = load_dataset("code_search_net", "python", split="train")
    csn_problems_raw = [
        x
        for x in csn_ds
        if x.get("func_code_string") and len(x["func_code_string"]) < 500
    ][:60]
    print(f"CodeSearchNet loaded: {len(csn_problems_raw)} problems")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"CodeSearchNet load error: {e}")
    csn_problems_raw = []


def parse_assert_tests(assert_list, fn_name=None):
    tests = []
    for stmt in assert_list:
        stmt = stmt.strip()
        m = re.match(r"assert\s+(.+?)\s*==\s*(.+?)(?:\s*,.*)?$", stmt)
        if m:
            call_str = m.group(1).strip()
            exp_str = m.group(2).strip()
            try:
                expected = eval(exp_str)
                tests.append((call_str, expected))
            except Exception:
                pass
    return tests


def parse_humaneval_tests(test_code, fn_name):
    tests = []
    patterns = [
        rf"assert\s+candidate\s*\((.+?)\)\s*==\s*(.+?)(?:\s*,.*)?$",
        rf"assert\s+{re.escape(fn_name)}\s*\((.+?)\)\s*==\s*(.+?)(?:\s*,.*)?$",
    ]
    for line in test_code.split("\n"):
        line = line.strip()
        for pat in patterns:
            m = re.match(pat, line)
            if m:
                args_str = m.group(1).strip()
                exp_str = m.group(2).strip()
                try:
                    expected = eval(exp_str)
                    call_str = f"{fn_name}({args_str})"
                    tests.append((call_str, expected))
                    break
                except Exception:
                    pass
    for m in re.finditer(rf"candidate\s*\(([^)]+)\)\s*==\s*([^\n,)]+)", test_code):
        args_str = m.group(1).strip()
        exp_str = m.group(2).strip()
        try:
            expected = eval(exp_str)
            call_str = f"{fn_name}({args_str})"
            if (call_str, expected) not in tests:
                tests.append((call_str, expected))
        except Exception:
            pass
    return tests


def make_adversarial_from_tests(fn_name, tests):
    if not tests:
        return [
            f"def {fn_name}(*args, **kwargs):\n    return None",
            f"def {fn_name}(*args, **kwargs):\n    return 0",
        ]
    entries = []
    for call_str, expected in tests:
        m = re.match(rf"{re.escape(fn_name)}\s*\((.*)\)$", call_str, re.DOTALL)
        if m:
            args_str = m.group(1).strip()
            try:
                exp_repr = repr(expected)
                entries.append(f"    {args_str}: {exp_repr}")
            except Exception:
                pass
    if entries:
        adv1_lines = [f"def {fn_name}(*args):"]
        adv1_lines.append("    _lookup = {")
        for e in entries:
            adv1_lines.append(f"        {e},")
        adv1_lines.append("    }")
        adv1_lines.append("    _key = args[0] if len(args)==1 else args")
        adv1_lines.append("    return _lookup.get(_key, None)")
        adv1 = "\n".join(adv1_lines)
    else:
        adv1 = f"def {fn_name}(*args, **kwargs):\n    return None"
    first_exp = tests[0][1] if tests else None
    adv2 = f"def {fn_name}(*args, **kwargs):\n    return {repr(first_exp)}"
    return [adv1, adv2]


def make_humaneval_problems(raw_items):
    problems = []
    for i, item in enumerate(raw_items):
        spec = item.get("prompt", "")[:300]
        code = item.get("canonical_solution", "")
        fn_name = item.get("entry_point", "fn")
        test_code = item.get("test", "")
        all_tests = parse_humaneval_tests(test_code, fn_name)
        if not all_tests:
            assert_stmts = re.findall(r"assert\s+.+", test_code)
            all_tests = parse_assert_tests(assert_stmts, fn_name)
        mid = max(1, len(all_tests) // 2)
        given_tests = all_tests[:mid]
        held_out_tests = all_tests[mid:] if len(all_tests) > mid else all_tests[:1]
        if not given_tests:
            continue
        adversarial_codes = make_adversarial_from_tests(fn_name, given_tests)
        problems.append(
            {
                "id": f"humaneval_{i}",
                "dataset": "humaneval",
                "spec": spec,
                "given_tests": given_tests,
                "held_out_tests": held_out_tests,
                "correct_code": item.get("prompt", "") + code,
                "adversarial_codes": adversarial_codes,
                "fn_name": fn_name,
            }
        )
    return problems


def make_mbpp_problems(raw_items):
    problems = []
    for i, item in enumerate(raw_items):
        spec = item.get("text", "")[:300]
        code = item.get("code", "")
        test_list = item.get("test_list", [])
        fn_name = "fn"
        m = re.search(r"def (\w+)\s*\(", code)
        if m:
            fn_name = m.group(1)
        all_tests = parse_assert_tests(test_list, fn_name)
        mid = max(1, len(all_tests) // 2)
        given_tests = all_tests[:mid]
        held_out_tests = all_tests[mid:] if len(all_tests) > mid else all_tests[:1]
        if not given_tests:
            continue
        adversarial_codes = make_adversarial_from_tests(fn_name, given_tests)
        problems.append(
            {
                "id": f"mbpp_{i}",
                "dataset": "mbpp",
                "spec": spec,
                "given_tests": given_tests,
                "held_out_tests": held_out_tests,
                "correct_code": code,
                "adversarial_codes": adversarial_codes,
                "fn_name": fn_name,
            }
        )
    return problems


def make_csn_problems(raw_items):
    problems = []
    for i, item in enumerate(raw_items):
        code = item.get("func_code_string", "")
        fn_name = item.get("func_name", "fn").split(".")[-1]
        spec = item.get("func_documentation_string", "")[:300]
        if not code or not fn_name:
            continue
        sig_match = re.search(rf"def {re.escape(fn_name)}\s*\(([^)]*)\)", code)
        if not sig_match:
            continue
        params = sig_match.group(1).strip()
        if not params or params in ("self", "*args", "**kwargs"):
            continue
        param_list = [
            p.strip().split(":")[0].strip().split("=")[0].strip()
            for p in params.split(",")
            if p.strip() and p.strip() != "self"
        ]
        if not param_list or len(param_list) > 2:
            continue
        namespace = {}
        try:
            exec(code, namespace)
            fn = namespace.get(fn_name)
            if fn is None:
                continue
        except Exception:
            continue
        input_sets = []
        for inp_str in [
            "'hello'",
            "'test'",
            "'python'",
            "'world'",
            "'abc'",
            "'foo'",
            "'bar'",
        ]:
            try:
                inp_val = eval(inp_str)
                result = fn(inp_val)
                if result is not None:
                    input_sets.append((f"{fn_name}({inp_str})", result))
            except Exception:
                pass
        if len(input_sets) < 2:
            for inp_str in ["1", "2", "3", "5", "10", "0", "4"]:
                try:
                    inp_val = eval(inp_str)
                    result = fn(inp_val)
                    if result is not None:
                        input_sets.append((f"{fn_name}({inp_str})", result))
                except Exception:
                    pass
        if len(input_sets) < 2:
            for inp_str in ["[1,2,3]", "[0]", "[1,2]", "[3,4,5]", "[]", "[10,20]"]:
                try:
                    inp_val = eval(inp_str)
                    result = fn(inp_val)
                    if result is not None:
                        input_sets.append((f"{fn_name}({inp_str})", result))
                except Exception:
                    pass
        seen = set()
        unique_sets = []
        for item2 in input_sets:
            key = (item2[0], repr(item2[1]))
            if key not in seen:
                seen.add(key)
                unique_sets.append(item2)
        input_sets = unique_sets
        if len(input_sets) < 2:
            continue
        mid = max(1, len(input_sets) // 2)
        given_tests = input_sets[:mid]
        held_out_tests = input_sets[mid:] if len(input_sets) > mid else input_sets[:1]
        adversarial_codes = make_adversarial_from_tests(fn_name, given_tests)
        problems.append(
            {
                "id": f"csn_{i}",
                "dataset": "csn",
                "spec": spec,
                "given_tests": given_tests,
                "held_out_tests": held_out_tests,
                "correct_code": code,
                "adversarial_codes": adversarial_codes,
                "fn_name": fn_name,
            }
        )
    return problems


hf_humaneval_probs = make_humaneval_problems(humaneval_problems_raw)
hf_mbpp_probs = make_mbpp_problems(mbpp_problems_raw)
hf_csn_probs = make_csn_problems(csn_problems_raw)

print(
    f"Parsed problems: HumanEval={len(hf_humaneval_probs)}, MBPP={len(hf_mbpp_probs)}, CSN={len(hf_csn_probs)}"
)

SYNTHETIC_PROBLEMS = [

            "def longest_word(s):\n    d={'hello world':5,'I am here':4,'cat':3}\n    return d.get(s,0)",

]

VOCAB_TOKENS = [

]
VOCAB = {tok: i for i, tok in enumerate(VOCAB_TOKENS)}
VOCAB_SIZE = len(VOCAB)
MAX_LEN = 128


def tokenize_code(code_str, max_len=MAX_LEN):
    tokens = re.findall(r"\w+|[^\w\s]", code_str)
    ids = [VOCAB.get(tok, VOCAB["<UNK>"]) for tok in tokens]
    ids = ids[:max_len]
    ids += [VOCAB["<PAD>"]] * (max_len - len(ids))
    return ids


class SemanticDiscriminator(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self, vocab_size=VOCAB_SIZE, embed_dim=64, hidden_dim=256, dropout=0.1
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, embed_dim, padding_idx=0)
        self.encoder = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(embed_dim, hidden_dim),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, hidden_dim),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, hidden_dim // 2),
            nn.GELU(),
        )
        self.classifier = nn.Linear(hidden_dim // 2, 1)

    def forward(self, token_ids):
        emb = self.embedding(token_ids)
        pooled = emb.mean(dim=1)
        h = self.encoder(pooled)
        return self.classifier(h).squeeze(-1)


class CodeAlignmentDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, samples):
        self.samples = samples

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.samples)

    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        ids, label = self.samples[idx]
        return torch.tensor(ids, dtype=torch.long), torch.tensor(
            label, dtype=torch.float
        )


def build_discriminator_dataset(problems):
    samples = []
    for prob in problems:
        if prob.get("correct_code"):
            samples.append((tokenize_code(prob["correct_code"]), 1))
        for adv in prob.get("adversarial_codes", []):
            samples.append((tokenize_code(adv), 0))
    return samples


all_problems = SYNTHETIC_PROBLEMS + hf_humaneval_probs + hf_mbpp_probs + hf_csn_probs
all_samples = build_discriminator_dataset(all_problems)
random.shuffle(all_samples)
split = int(0.75 * len(all_samples))
train_samples = all_samples[:split]
val_samples = all_samples[split:]
train_dataset = CodeAlignmentDataset(train_samples)
val_dataset = CodeAlignmentDataset(val_samples)
train_loader = DataLoader(train_dataset, batch_size=16, shuffle=True)
val_loader = DataLoader(val_dataset, batch_size=16, shuffle=False)
print(f"Discriminator dataset: {len(train_samples)} train, {len(val_samples)} val")


def safe_exec(code_str, test_call, expected):
    try:
        namespace = {}
        exec(code_str, namespace)
        result = eval(test_call, namespace)
        return result == expected
    except Exception:
        return False


def evaluate_code(code_str, tests):
    if not tests:
        return 0.0
    return sum(safe_exec(code_str, call, exp) for call, exp in tests) / len(tests)


def execution_reward(code_str, given_tests):
    return evaluate_code(code_str, given_tests)


def semantic_reward_fn(code_str, discriminator, dev):
    discriminator.eval()
    ids = tokenize_code(code_str)
    t = torch.tensor([ids], dtype=torch.long).to(dev)
    with torch.no_grad():
        logit = discriminator(t)
        prob = torch.sigmoid(logit).item()
    return prob


def get_lambda_for_epoch(epoch, num_epochs, schedule_type, base_lam=0.3):
    """
    Compute effective lambda for a given epoch based on the temporal discounting schedule.
    - 'flat': constant lambda (baseline)
    - 'delayed': lambda=0 for first 50%, then ramps up to base_lam
    - 'decay': starts at base_lam, decays exponentially toward 0 (semantics early, execution later)
    - 'growth': starts at 0, grows exponentially toward base_lam (execution early, semantics later)
    """
    t = epoch / num_epochs  # normalized time in (0, 1]
    if schedule_type == "flat":
        return base_lam
    elif schedule_type == "delayed":
        warmup_frac = 0.5
        if t < warmup_frac:
            return 0.0
        else:
            ramp = (t - warmup_frac) / (1.0 - warmup_frac)
            return base_lam * ramp
    elif schedule_type == "decay":
        return base_lam * np.exp(-5.0 * t)
    elif schedule_type == "growth":
        return base_lam * (1.0 - np.exp(-5.0 * t))
    return base_lam


def combined_reward_dynamic(code_str, given_tests, discriminator, dev, effective_lam):
    exec_r = execution_reward(code_str, given_tests)
    if effective_lam == 0.0:
        return exec_r
    sem_r = semantic_reward_fn(code_str, discriminator, dev)
    return (1.0 - effective_lam) * exec_r + effective_lam * sem_r


def get_candidate_pool(prob):
    return [prob["correct_code"]] + prob.get("adversarial_codes", [])


def train_discriminator(num_epochs=30, lr=1e-3):
    disc = SemanticDiscriminator().to(device)
    optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(disc.parameters(), lr=lr)
    criterion = nn.BCEWithLogitsLoss()
    train_losses, val_losses, val_accs = [], [], []
    for epoch in range(1, num_epochs + 1):
        disc.train()
        train_loss = 0.0
        for ids_b, labels_b in train_loader:
            ids_b, labels_b = ids_b.to(device), labels_b.to(device)
            optimizer.zero_grad()
            logits = disc(ids_b)
            loss = criterion(logits, labels_b)
            loss.backward()
            optimizer.step()
            train_loss += loss.item()
        train_loss /= max(len(train_loader), 1)
        disc.eval()
        val_loss, all_preds, all_labels = 0.0, [], []
        with torch.no_grad():
            for ids_b, labels_b in val_loader:
                ids_b, labels_b = ids_b.to(device), labels_b.to(device)
                logits = disc(ids_b)
                val_loss += criterion(logits, labels_b).item()
                preds = (torch.sigmoid(logits) > 0.5).cpu().numpy()
                all_preds.extend(preds)
                all_labels.extend(labels_b.cpu().numpy())
        val_loss /= max(len(val_loader), 1)
        val_acc = accuracy_score(all_labels, all_preds) if all_labels else 0.0
        train_losses.append(train_loss)
        val_losses.append(val_loss)
        val_accs.append(val_acc)
        if epoch % 5 == 0:
            print(
                f"  Disc Epoch {epoch}: val_loss={val_loss:.4f}, val_acc={val_acc:.4f}"
            )
    return disc, np.array(train_losses), np.array(val_losses), np.array(val_accs)


def run_rl_experiment_temporal(
    problems, discriminator, num_epochs=25, schedule_type="flat", base_lam=0.3, seed=42
):
    """RL experiment with temporal discounting of reward signals."""
    np.random.seed(seed)
    given_all, held_all, sgg_all, lambda_schedule = [], [], [], []
    valid_problems = [
        p for p in problems if p.get("given_tests") and p.get("held_out_tests")
    ]
    if not valid_problems:
        return (
            np.zeros(num_epochs),
            np.zeros(num_epochs),
            np.zeros(num_epochs),
            np.zeros(num_epochs),
        )
    for epoch in range(1, num_epochs + 1):
        noise_scale = max(0.05, 0.4 - epoch * 0.015)
        effective_lam = get_lambda_for_epoch(epoch, num_epochs, schedule_type, base_lam)
        lambda_schedule.append(effective_lam)
        given_rates, held_rates = [], []
        for prob in valid_problems:
            candidates = get_candidate_pool(prob)
            scores = [
                combined_reward_dynamic(
                    c, prob["given_tests"], discriminator, device, effective_lam
                )
                + np.random.normal(0, noise_scale)
                for c in candidates
            ]
            best_code = candidates[np.argmax(scores)]
            given_rates.append(evaluate_code(best_code, prob["given_tests"]))
            held_rates.append(evaluate_code(best_code, prob["held_out_tests"]))
        g_mean = np.mean(given_rates) if given_rates else 0.0
        h_mean = np.mean(held_rates) if held_rates else 0.0
        given_all.append(g_mean)
        held_all.append(h_mean)
        sgg_all.append(g_mean - h_mean)
    return (
        np.array(given_all),
        np.array(held_all),
        np.array(sgg_all),
        np.array(lambda_schedule),
    )

            f"{ds_name:<30} {SCHEDULE_LABELS[sched]:<30} {final_sgg:<12.4f} {final_held:<14.4f}"
        )
    print()

print("\n=== Epoch-level Lambda and SGG (every 5 epochs, first dataset) ===")
ds_name = ds_list[0]
print(f"\nDataset: {ds_name}")
for sched in SCHEDULE_TYPES:
    sgg_arr = experiment_data[sched][ds_name]["sgg"]
    lam_arr = experiment_data[sched][ds_name]["lambda_schedule"]
    print(f"  Schedule={sched}:")
    for ep in range(NUM_RL_EPOCHS):
        if (ep + 1) % 5 == 0:
            print(f"    Epoch {ep+1}: λ={lam_arr[ep]:.4f}, SGG={sgg_arr[ep]:.4f}")

print(
    f"\nDiscriminator: val_acc={disc_val_acc[-1]:.4f}, val_loss={disc_val_loss[-1]:.4f}"
)
print("\n=== Experiment Complete ===")
plot_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")

SCHEDULE_TYPES = ["flat", "delayed", "decay", "growth"]
SCHEDULE_LABELS = {
    "flat": "Flat (λ=0.3 constant)",
    "delayed": "Delayed (warmup 50%)",
    "decay": "Decay (semantics→exec)",
    "growth": "Growth (exec→semantics)",
}
SCHEDULE_COLORS = {
    "flat": "blue",
    "delayed": "orange",
    "decay": "red",
    "growth": "green",
}
ds_list = ["hf_humaneval", "hf_mbpp", "hf_csn"]

# Plot 1: Discriminator training curves
try:
    disc_data = experiment_data["discriminator"]
    train_loss = disc_data["train_loss"]
    val_loss = disc_data["val_loss"]
    val_acc = disc_data["val_acc"]
    epochs_disc = list(range(1, len(train_loss) + 1))
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 5))
    axes[0].plot(epochs_disc, train_loss, label="Train Loss", color="blue")
    axes[0].plot(epochs_disc, val_loss, label="Val Loss", color="orange")
    axes[0].set_xlabel("Epoch")
    axes[0].set_ylabel("BCE Loss")
    axes[0].set_title("Discriminator Training & Validation Loss")
    axes[0].legend()
    axes[0].grid(True)
    axes[1].plot(epochs_disc, val_acc, label="Val Accuracy", color="green")
    axes[1].set_xlabel("Epoch")
    axes[1].set_ylabel("Accuracy")
    axes[1].set_title("Discriminator Validation Accuracy")
    axes[1].legend()
    axes[1].grid(True)
    plt.suptitle(
        "Semantic Discriminator Training (Correct vs Adversarial Code)", fontsize=12
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, "discriminator_training_curves.png"), dpi=100)
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: discriminator_training_curves.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot1: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 2: Lambda schedule visualization
try:
    sample_ds = ds_list[0]
    lam_data = {
        s: experiment_data[s][sample_ds]["lambda_schedule"] for s in SCHEDULE_TYPES
    }
    epochs_rl = list(range(1, len(lam_data["flat"]) + 1))
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
    for sched in SCHEDULE_TYPES:
        ax.plot(
            epochs_rl,
            lam_data[sched],
            label=SCHEDULE_LABELS[sched],
            color=SCHEDULE_COLORS[sched],
            linewidth=2,
        )
    ax.set_xlabel("RL Epoch")
    ax.set_ylabel("Effective λ (semantic weight)")
    ax.set_title(
        "Temporal Lambda Schedules (base λ=0.3)\nShowing how semantic reward weight changes over training"
    )
    ax.legend()
    ax.grid(True)
    ax.set_ylim(-0.02, 0.4)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "lambda_schedules_visualization.png"), dpi=100
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: lambda_schedules_visualization.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot2: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 3: Given Pass Rate curves per dataset per schedule
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
    for ax_i, ds_name in enumerate(ds_list):
        ax = axes[ax_i]
        for sched in SCHEDULE_TYPES:
            g = experiment_data[sched][ds_name]["given_pass_rate"]
            epochs_rl = list(range(1, len(g) + 1))
            ax.plot(
                epochs_rl,
                g,
                label=SCHEDULE_LABELS[sched],
                color=SCHEDULE_COLORS[sched],
                linewidth=2,
            )
        ax.set_xlabel("RL Epoch")
        ax.set_ylabel("Given Pass Rate")
        ax.set_title(f"Given (Training) Pass Rate\nDataset: {ds_name}")
        ax.legend(fontsize=7)
        ax.grid(True)
        ax.set_ylim(-0.05, 1.1)
    plt.suptitle("Given Pass Rate by Temporal Schedule Across Datasets", fontsize=12)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "given_pass_rate_by_schedule_all_datasets.png"),
        dpi=100,
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: given_pass_rate_by_schedule_all_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot3: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 4: Held-out Pass Rate curves per dataset per schedule
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
    for ax_i, ds_name in enumerate(ds_list):
        ax = axes[ax_i]
        for sched in SCHEDULE_TYPES:
            h = experiment_data[sched][ds_name]["held_out_pass_rate"]
            epochs_rl = list(range(1, len(h) + 1))
            ax.plot(
                epochs_rl,
                h,
                label=SCHEDULE_LABELS[sched],
                color=SCHEDULE_COLORS[sched],
                linewidth=2,
            )
        ax.set_xlabel("RL Epoch")
        ax.set_ylabel("Held-out Pass Rate")
        ax.set_title(f"Held-out (Test) Pass Rate\nDataset: {ds_name}")
        ax.legend(fontsize=7)
        ax.grid(True)
        ax.set_ylim(-0.05, 1.1)
    plt.suptitle("Held-out Pass Rate by Temporal Schedule Across Datasets", fontsize=12)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "held_out_pass_rate_by_schedule_all_datasets.png"),
        dpi=100,
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: held_out_pass_rate_by_schedule_all_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot4: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 5: SGG curves per dataset per schedule
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
    for ax_i, ds_name in enumerate(ds_list):
        ax = axes[ax_i]
        for sched in SCHEDULE_TYPES:
            sgg = experiment_data[sched][ds_name]["sgg"]
            epochs_rl = list(range(1, len(sgg) + 1))
            ax.plot(
                epochs_rl,
                sgg,
                label=SCHEDULE_LABELS[sched],
                color=SCHEDULE_COLORS[sched],
                linewidth=2,
            )
        ax.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle=":", alpha=0.5)
        ax.set_xlabel("RL Epoch")
        ax.set_ylabel("SGG (Given - Held-out Pass Rate)")
        ax.set_title(f"Semantic Generalization Gap (SGG)\nDataset: {ds_name}")
        ax.legend(fontsize=7)
        ax.grid(True)
    plt.suptitle(
        "SGG by Temporal Schedule (lower SGG = better generalization)", fontsize=12
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "sgg_curves_by_schedule_all_datasets.png"), dpi=100
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: sgg_curves_by_schedule_all_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot5: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 6: Final SGG bar chart (last 5 epochs avg)
try:
    n_ds = len(ds_list)
    n_bars = len(SCHEDULE_TYPES)
    width = 0.2
    x = np.arange(n_ds)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 6))
    for bi, sched in enumerate(SCHEDULE_TYPES):
        vals = [
            float(np.mean(experiment_data[sched][ds]["sgg"][-5:])) for ds in ds_list
        ]
        offset = (bi - n_bars / 2 + 0.5) * width
        bars = ax.bar(
            x + offset,
            vals,
            width,
            label=SCHEDULE_LABELS[sched],
            color=SCHEDULE_COLORS[sched],
            alpha=0.8,
        )
        for bar in bars:
            h_val = bar.get_height()
            ax.text(
                bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
                h_val + 0.005,
                f"{h_val:.3f}",
                ha="center",
                va="bottom",
                fontsize=7,
            )
    ax.set_xticks(x)
    ax.set_xticklabels([d.replace("hf_", "").upper() for d in ds_list], fontsize=10)
    ax.set_ylabel("Final SGG (last 5 epochs avg)")
    ax.set_title(
        "Temporal Reward Discounting Ablation\nFinal Semantic Generalization Gap by Schedule and Dataset\n(lower = better generalization to held-out tests)"
    )
    ax.legend(fontsize=9)
    ax.grid(True, axis="y")
    ax.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "final_sgg_bar_chart_all_datasets.png"), dpi=100
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: final_sgg_bar_chart_all_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot6: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 7: SGG Heatmap
try:
    sgg_matrix = np.zeros((len(SCHEDULE_TYPES), n_ds))
    for si, sched in enumerate(SCHEDULE_TYPES):
        for di, ds_name in enumerate(ds_list):
            sgg_matrix[si, di] = float(
                np.mean(experiment_data[sched][ds_name]["sgg"][-5:])
            )
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
    im = ax.imshow(sgg_matrix, cmap="RdYlGn_r", aspect="auto", vmin=-0.1, vmax=0.5)
    ax.set_xticks(range(n_ds))
    ax.set_xticklabels([d.replace("hf_", "").upper() for d in ds_list], fontsize=10)
    ax.set_yticks(range(len(SCHEDULE_TYPES)))
    ax.set_yticklabels([SCHEDULE_LABELS[s] for s in SCHEDULE_TYPES], fontsize=9)
    for i in range(len(SCHEDULE_TYPES)):
        for j in range(n_ds):
            ax.text(
                j,
                i,
                f"{sgg_matrix[i, j]:.3f}",
                ha="center",
                va="center",
                fontsize=11,
                color="black",
            )
    plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label="SGG (lower=better generalization)")
    ax.set_title(
        "SGG Heatmap: Temporal Reward Schedule vs Dataset\n(Final 5-epoch average, lower SGG = better)"
    )
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "sgg_heatmap_schedule_vs_dataset.png"), dpi=100
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: sgg_heatmap_schedule_vs_dataset.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot7: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Plot 8: Final Held-out Pass Rate bar chart
try:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 6))
    for bi, sched in enumerate(SCHEDULE_TYPES):
        vals = [
            float(np.mean(experiment_data[sched][ds]["held_out_pass_rate"][-5:]))
            for ds in ds_list
        ]
        offset = (bi - n_bars / 2 + 0.5) * width
        bars = ax.bar(
            x + offset,
            vals,
            width,
            label=SCHEDULE_LABELS[sched],
            color=SCHEDULE_COLORS[sched],
            alpha=0.8,
        )
        for bar in bars:
            h_val = bar.get_height()
            ax.text(
                bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
                h_val + 0.005,
                f"{h_val:.3f}",
                ha="center",
                va="bottom",
                fontsize=7,
            )
    ax.set_xticks(x)
    ax.set_xticklabels([d.replace("hf_", "").upper() for d in ds_list], fontsize=10)
    ax.set_ylabel("Final Held-out Pass Rate (last 5 epochs avg)")
    ax.set_title(
        "Temporal Reward Discounting Ablation\nFinal Held-out Pass Rate by Schedule and Dataset\n(higher = better generalization)"
    )
    ax.legend(fontsize=9)
    ax.grid(True, axis="y")
    ax.set_ylim(0, 1.15)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "final_held_out_pass_rate_bar_all_datasets.png"),
        dpi=100,
    )
    plt.close()
    print("Saved: final_held_out_pass_rate_bar_all_datasets.png")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error plot8: {e}")
    plt.close()

# Print summary metrics
try:
    print("\n=== Summary Metrics ===")
    print(f'{"Dataset":<15} {"Schedule":<30} {"Final SGG":>10} {"Held-out PR":>12}')
    print("-" * 70)
    for ds_name in ds_list:
        for sched in SCHEDULE_TYPES:
            fsgg = float(np.mean(experiment_data[sched][ds_name]["sgg"][-5:]))
            fhpr = float(
                np.mean(experiment_data[sched][ds_name]["held_out_pass_rate"][-5:])
            )
            print(
                f"{ds_name:<15} {SCHEDULE_LABELS[sched]:<30} {fsgg:>10.4f} {fhpr:>12.4f}"
            )
        print()
    disc = experiment_data["discriminator"]
    print(
        f'Discriminator final val_acc={disc["val_acc"][-1]:.4f}, val_loss={disc["val_loss"][-1]:.4f}'
    )
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error summary: {e}")
plot_plan: The experiment trains a semantic discriminator to distinguish correct vs adversarial code, then runs RL experiments across 3 datasets (HumanEval, MBPP, CodeSearchNet) using 4 temporal lambda schedules (flat, delayed, decay, growth) to control the semantic reward weighting over training epochs. Key metrics are: discriminator train/val loss and accuracy, given/held-out pass rates per schedule per dataset, semantic generalization gap (SGG = given - held-out pass rate), lambda schedule curves, and summary bar charts/heatmaps. I'll plot the discriminator training curves, lambda schedule visualization, per-dataset given/held-out pass rate curves per schedule, SGG curves, a bar chart of final SGG, a heatmap of SGG across schedules and datasets, and a final held-out pass rate bar chart. Each plot will be in its own try-except block, saved with descriptive names, and figures closed after saving.

plot_analyses.analysis: Temporal λ schedules: four schedules with base λ=0.3 over 25 RL epochs. Flat stays at 0.3. Delayed keeps λ≈0 until ~epoch 12–13 then linearly ramps to 0.3 by epoch 25. Decay starts high (~0.25) and exponentially decays toward ~0 by epoch 25 (semantics→exec). Growth starts low (~0.05) and asymptotically increases toward 0.3 by epoch 25 (exec→semantics). This sets up a clean test of when to apply semantic reward pressure.

Discriminator training: Train BCE loss drops from ~0.68 to ~0 by ~epoch 20–30, while validation loss drops quickly early (~0.6→~0.18 by epoch 3) then plateaus ~0.11–0.14 with a noticeable spike around epochs ~21–25 (~0.2) before returning ~0.11. Validation accuracy rises from ~0.946 to ~0.982 by ~epoch 6 and stays flat. Interpretation: discriminator becomes extremely confident on train (near-perfect fit) with stable high val accuracy; the val-loss spike suggests occasional calibration/overconfidence or distribution shift in val minibatches. For RL reward shaping, this implies the semantic score may saturate; consider temperature scaling or margin-based reward to avoid vanishing gradients / reward cliffs.

SGG over RL epochs (HumanEval/MBPP/CSN): HumanEval—Flat and Growth trend downward and hit ~0 by ~epoch 15–22; Delayed remains higher early and only approaches ~0 late; Decay stays high/volatile (~0.1–0.35) and does not converge to 0. MBPP—Flat and Growth decrease to ~0 by ~epoch 18–22; Delayed starts high (~0.45–0.5) and gradually decreases to ~0 by ~epoch 22–25; Decay remains large (~0.25–0.48) and even increases late. CSN—SGG is identically 0 across all epochs/schedules, consistent with earlier ‘CSN always 0’ issue; likely metric degeneracy due to both given and held-out pass rates being 0 (confirmed in later plots) or missing held-out tests.

Held-out pass rate over RL epochs: HumanEval—Flat and Growth climb to ~0.95–0.97 by late epochs; Delayed catches up late (~0.95–0.97); Decay underperforms and trends ~0.7–0.8. MBPP—Flat and Growth reach ~0.99–1.0 late; Delayed improves steadily to ~0.98–1.0 late; Decay collapses to ~0.52–0.75 and trends downward. CSN—held-out pass rate is 0 for all schedules/epochs, reinforcing a dataset/eval pipeline failure rather than a schedule effect.

Given (training) pass rate over RL epochs: HumanEval—near-saturated ~0.95–0.98 across all schedules with minimal separation. MBPP—essentially 1.0 throughout for all schedules. CSN—0 throughout. Key implication: schedules mainly affect generalization (held-out) and SGG, not given-test performance, which is already saturated; thus SGG differences are meaningful and not confounded by training-test failure—except CSN where everything is 0.

SGG heatmap (final 5-epoch avg): HumanEval—Flat 0.006, Delayed 0.013, Decay 0.222, Growth 0.006. MBPP—Flat 0.007, Delayed 0.013, Decay 0.440, Growth 0.007. CSN—0.000 across all. Interpretation: Decay schedule is dramatically worse (higher SGG = worse generalization) on both HumanEval and MBPP; Flat and Growth are best and essentially tied; Delayed is slightly worse but still very low SGG (<0.05 success criterion).

Bar chart: final SGG by schedule/dataset confirms heatmap numerically; Decay is the only schedule violating SGG<0.05 (0.222/0.440). Flat/Growth meet criterion strongly; Delayed meets criterion but with ~2× SGG vs Flat/Growth (still small in absolute terms).

Bar chart: final held-out pass rate by schedule/dataset: HumanEval—Flat 0.961, Delayed 0.955, Decay 0.746, Growth 0.961. MBPP—Flat 0.993, Delayed 0.987, Decay 0.560, Growth 0.993. CSN—0.0 all. Interpretation: Flat and Growth maximize held-out pass rate; Delayed slightly lower; Decay severely harms held-out performance. Combined with SGG, Flat/Growth dominate Pareto frontier here.

Duplicate/alternate bar charts reiterate the same final SGG and held-out pass rate results (same values). This redundancy strengthens confidence that the reported last-5-epoch averages are consistent.

vlm_feedback_summary: Across HumanEval and MBPP, temporal scheduling of the semantic reward weight matters: Flat λ=0.3 and Growth (exec→semantics) achieve the best generalization (SGG ≈0.006–0.007) and the highest held-out pass rates (≈0.961 HumanEval, ≈0.993 MBPP). Delayed warmup reaches similar endpoints but is slightly worse (SGG ≈0.013; held-out slightly lower). Decay (semantics→exec) is consistently harmful, producing large SGG (0.222/0.440) and much lower held-out pass (0.746/0.560), despite given-test pass being saturated—indicative of increased test-overfitting or destabilized learning when semantics is emphasized early then removed. CSN shows 0 given and 0 held-out pass and thus 0 SGG for all schedules, pointing to an evaluation/data-format issue rather than a modeling effect. Discriminator training achieves very high validation accuracy (~0.982) with near-zero train loss and a mild val-loss spike mid-training, suggesting potential calibration/saturation issues for reward shaping.
ablation_name: Reward Temporal Discounting - Immediate vs Delayed vs Exponential Decay Reward Signals

overall_plan:
Ablation name: Discriminator Feedback Frequency - Continuous vs Episodic vs Milestone-Based Updates.
The ablation study tests 6 discriminator feedback frequency strategies (no_disc, continuous, every_5, every_10, milestone_acc, warmup_ramp) across 3 HuggingFace datasets. The core modification is the `run_rl_feedback_frequency` function that computes an `effective_lam` per RL epoch based on the chosen strategy, with milestone-based gating using the discriminator's validation accuracy trajectory to determine when it becomes reliable enough to influence selection. Results are saved as `experiment_data.npy` and visualized through 6 plots showing discriminator training curves, effective lambda schedules, SGG curves, held-out pass rates, bar charts, and a heatmap summary.

import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, Dataset
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import random
import re
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Using device: {device}")

random.seed(42)
np.random.seed(42)
torch.manual_seed(42)

from datasets import load_dataset

print("Loading HuggingFace datasets...")
try:
    humaneval_ds = load_dataset("openai_humaneval", split="test")
    humaneval_problems_raw = list(humaneval_ds)[:40]
    print(f"HumanEval loaded: {len(humaneval_problems_raw)} problems")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"HumanEval load error: {e}")
    humaneval_problems_raw = []

try:
    mbpp_ds = load_dataset("mbpp", split="test")
    mbpp_problems_raw = list(mbpp_ds)[:40]
    print(f"MBPP loaded: {len(mbpp_problems_raw)} problems")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"MBPP load error: {e}")
    mbpp_problems_raw = []

try:
    csn_ds = load_dataset("code_search_net", "python", split="train")
    csn_problems_raw = [
        x
        for x in csn_ds
        if x.get("func_code_string") and len(x["func_code_string"]) < 500
    ][:60]
    print(f"CodeSearchNet loaded: {len(csn_problems_raw)} problems")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"CodeSearchNet load error: {e}")
    csn_problems_raw = []


def parse_assert_tests(assert_list, fn_name=None):
    tests = []
    for stmt in assert_list:
        stmt = stmt.strip()
        m = re.match(r"assert\s+(.+?)\s*==\s*(.+?)(?:\s*,.*)?$", stmt)
        if m:
            call_str = m.group(1).strip()
            exp_str = m.group(2).strip()
            try:
                expected = eval(exp_str)
                tests.append((call_str, expected))
            except Exception:
                pass
    return tests


def parse_humaneval_tests(test_code, fn_name):
    tests = []
    patterns = [
        rf"assert\s+candidate\s*\((.+?)\)\s*==\s*(.+?)(?:\s*,.*)?$",
        rf"assert\s+{re.escape(fn_name)}\s*\((.+?)\)\s*==\s*(.+?)(?:\s*,.*)?$",
    ]
    for line in test_code.split("\n"):
        line = line.strip()
        for pat in patterns:
            m = re.match(pat, line)
            if m:
                args_str = m.group(1).strip()
                exp_str = m.group(2).strip()
                try:
                    expected = eval(exp_str)
                    call_str = f"{fn_name}({args_str})"
                    tests.append((call_str, expected))
                    break
                except Exception:
                    pass
    for m in re.finditer(rf"candidate\s*\(([^)]+)\)\s*==\s*([^\n,)]+)", test_code):
        args_str = m.group(1).strip()
        exp_str = m.group(2).strip()
        try:
            expected = eval(exp_str)
            call_str = f"{fn_name}({args_str})"
            if (call_str, expected) not in tests:
                tests.append((call_str, expected))
        except Exception:
            pass
    return tests


def make_adversarial_from_tests(fn_name, tests):
    if not tests:
        return [
            f"def {fn_name}(*args, **kwargs):\n    return None",
            f"def {fn_name}(*args, **kwargs):\n    return 0",
        ]
    entries = []
    for call_str, expected in tests:
        m = re.match(rf"{re.escape(fn_name)}\s*\((.*)\)$", call_str, re.DOTALL)
        if m:
            args_str = m.group(1).strip()
            try:
                entries.append(f"    {args_str}: {repr(expected)}")
            except Exception:
                pass
    if entries:
        adv1_lines = [f"def {fn_name}(*args):"]
        adv1_lines.append("    _lookup = {")
        for e in entries:
            adv1_lines.append(f"        {e},")
        adv1_lines.append("    }")
        adv1_lines.append("    _key = args[0] if len(args)==1 else args")
        adv1_lines.append("    return _lookup.get(_key, None)")
        adv1 = "\n".join(adv1_lines)
    else:
        adv1 = f"def {fn_name}(*args, **kwargs):\n    return None"
    first_exp = tests[0][1] if tests else None
    adv2 = f"def {fn_name}(*args, **kwargs):\n    return {repr(first_exp)}"
    return [adv1, adv2]


def make_humaneval_problems(raw_items):
    problems = []
    for i, item in enumerate(raw_items):
        spec = item.get("prompt", "")[:300]
        code = item.get("canonical_solution", "")
        fn_name = item.get("entry_point", "fn")
        test_code = item.get("test", "")
        all_tests = parse_humaneval_tests(test_code, fn_name)
        if not all_tests:
            assert_stmts = re.findall(r"assert\s+.+", test_code)
            all_tests = parse_assert_tests(assert_stmts, fn_name)
        mid = max(1, len(all_tests) // 2)
        given_tests = all_tests[:mid]
        held_out_tests = all_tests[mid:] if len(all_tests) > mid else all_tests[:1]
        if not given_tests:
            continue
        adversarial_codes = make_adversarial_from_tests(fn_name, given_tests)
        problems.append(
            {
                "id": f"humaneval_{i}",
                "dataset": "humaneval",
                "spec": spec,
                "given_tests": given_tests,
                "held_out_tests": held_out_tests,
                "correct_code": item.get("prompt", "") + code,
                "adversarial_codes": adversarial_codes,
                "fn_name": fn_name,
            }
        )
    return problems


def make_mbpp_problems(raw_items):
    problems = []
    for i, item in enumerate(raw_items):
        spec = item.get("text", "")[:300]
        code = item.get("code", "")
        test_list = item.get("test_list", [])
        fn_name = "fn"
        m = re.search(r"def (\w+)\s*\(", code)
        if m:
            fn_name = m.group(1)
        all_tests = parse_assert_tests(test_list, fn_name)
        mid = max(1, len(all_tests) // 2)
        given_tests = all_tests[:mid]
        held_out_tests = all_tests[mid:] if len(all_tests) > mid else all_tests[:1]
        if not given_tests:
            continue
        adversarial_codes = make_adversarial_from_tests(fn_name, given_tests)
        problems.append(
            {
                "id": f"mbpp_{i}",
                "dataset": "mbpp",
                "spec": spec,
                "given_tests": given_tests,
                "held_out_tests": held_out_tests,
                "correct_code": code,
                "adversarial_codes": adversarial_codes,
                "fn_name": fn_name,
            }
        )
    return problems


def make_csn_problems(raw_items):
    problems = []
    for i, item in enumerate(raw_items):
        code = item.get("func_code_string", "")
        fn_name = item.get("func_name", "fn").split(".")[-1]
        spec = item.get("func_documentation_string", "")[:300]
        if not code or not fn_name:
            continue
        sig_match = re.search(rf"def {re.escape(fn_name)}\s*\(([^)]*)\)", code)
        if not sig_match:
            continue
        params = sig_match.group(1).strip()
        if not params or params in ("self", "*args", "**kwargs"):
            continue
        param_list = [
            p.strip().split(":")[0].strip().split("=")[0].strip()
            for p in params.split(",")
            if p.strip() and p.strip() != "self"
        ]
        if not param_list or len(param_list) > 2:
            continue
        namespace = {}
        try:
            exec(code, namespace)
            fn = namespace.get(fn_name)
            if fn is None:
                continue
        except Exception:
            continue
        input_sets = []
        for inp_str in [
            "'hello'",
            "'test'",
            "'python'",
            "'world'",
            "'abc'",
            "'foo'",
            "'bar'",
        ]:
            try:
                inp_val = eval(inp_str)
                result = fn(inp_val)
                if result is not None:
                    input_sets.append((f"{fn_name}({inp_str})", result))
            except Exception:
                pass
        if len(input_sets) < 2:
            for inp_str in ["1", "2", "3", "5", "10", "0", "4"]:
                try:
                    inp_val = eval(inp_str)
                    result = fn(inp_val)
                    if result is not None:
                        input_sets.append((f"{fn_name}({inp_str})", result))
                except Exception:
                    pass
        if len(input_sets) < 2:
            for inp_str in ["[1,2,3]", "[0]", "[1,2]", "[3,4,5]", "[]", "[10,20]"]:
                try:
                    inp_val = eval(inp_str)
                    result = fn(inp_val)
                    if result is not None:
                        input_sets.append((f"{fn_name}({inp_str})", result))
                except Exception:
                    pass
        seen = set()
        unique_sets = []
        for item2 in input_sets:
            key = (item2[0], repr(item2[1]))
            if key not in seen:
                seen.add(key)
                unique_sets.append(item2)
        input_sets = unique_sets
        if len(input_sets) < 2:
            continue
        mid = max(1, len(input_sets) // 2)
        given_tests = input_sets[:mid]
        held_out_tests = input_sets[mid:] if len(input_sets) > mid else input_sets[:1]
        adversarial_codes = make_adversarial_from_tests(fn_name, given_tests)
        problems.append(
            {
                "id": f"csn_{i}",
                "dataset": "csn",
                "spec": spec,
                "given_tests": given_tests,
                "held_out_tests": held_out_tests,
                "correct_code": code,
                "adversarial_codes": adversarial_codes,
                "fn_name": fn_name,
            }
        )
    return problems


hf_humaneval_probs = make_humaneval_problems(humaneval_problems_raw)
hf_mbpp_probs = make_mbpp_problems(mbpp_problems_raw)
hf_csn_probs = make_csn_problems(csn_problems_raw)
print(
    f"Parsed problems: HumanEval={len(hf_humaneval_probs)}, MBPP={len(hf_mbpp_probs)}, CSN={len(hf_csn_probs)}"
)

VOCAB = {tok: i for i, tok in enumerate(VOCAB_TOKENS)}
VOCAB_SIZE = len(VOCAB)
MAX_LEN = 128


def tokenize_code(code_str, max_len=MAX_LEN):
    tokens = re.findall(r"\w+|[^\w\s]", code_str)
    ids = [VOCAB.get(tok, VOCAB["<UNK>"]) for tok in tokens]
    ids = ids[:max_len]
    ids += [VOCAB["<PAD>"]] * (max_len - len(ids))
    return ids


class SemanticDiscriminator(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self, vocab_size=VOCAB_SIZE, embed_dim=64, hidden_dim=256, dropout=0.1
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, embed_dim, padding_idx=0)
        self.encoder = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(embed_dim, hidden_dim),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, hidden_dim),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, hidden_dim // 2),
            nn.GELU(),
        )
        self.classifier = nn.Linear(hidden_dim // 2, 1)

    def forward(self, token_ids):
        emb = self.embedding(token_ids)
        pooled = emb.mean(dim=1)
        h = self.encoder(pooled)
        return self.classifier(h).squeeze(-1)


class CodeAlignmentDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, samples):
        self.samples = samples

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.samples)

    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        ids, label = self.samples[idx]
        return torch.tensor(ids, dtype=torch.long), torch.tensor(
            label, dtype=torch.float
        )


def build_discriminator_dataset(problems):
    samples = []
    for prob in problems:
        if prob.get("correct_code"):
            samples.append((tokenize_code(prob["correct_code"]), 1))
        for adv in prob.get("adversarial_codes", []):
            samples.append((tokenize_code(adv), 0))
    return samples
